vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF (#635 child, needs-discussion shape): Phase 0 spec scaffolding RFC -- multi-phase plan with open design questions for generated per-phase task gates (e.g. spec:phase0, spec:pipeline). Should run discussion before swarm assignment per canonical #642 workflow comment.

plan.title: RFC: Phase 0 spec scaffolding -- generated per-phase task gates (needs-discussion)

narratives.Problem: The original #233 More Determinism initiative proposed `task spec:phase0` and `task spec:pipeline` as generated per-phase task gates -- one of the largest design questions in the determinism arc, with cross-cutting impact on speckit / interview / build skills, the spec validator, and the migrator. Per canonical #642 workflow comment locked decisions, this is too multi-phased and design-question-heavy to compress into a leaf plan.item under #233; it gets its own RFC vBRIEF that runs discussion before swarm assignment. The #233 vBRIEF references this RFC as the gate item for `task ci:local` and `task release` (which depend on the Phase 0 design outcome).
narratives.Overview: Author and run a Phase 0 spec scaffolding RFC. The RFC asks: how should per-phase gates (Phase 1 -> 2 spec approval, Phase 3 -> 4 spec render, Phase 4 implementation phase boundaries) be generated as runnable tasks rather than enforced by skill prose? Open design questions (non-exhaustive): (1) one task per spec or one task per phase? (2) where does the generated task live -- tasks/spec.yml, a generated tasks/spec-<project>.yml, or inline in vbrief/specification.vbrief.json metadata? (3) how do the gates interact with skills/deft-directive-build/SKILL.md and skills/deft-directive-interview/SKILL.md? (4) backwards compat with existing speckit Phase 4 emission? The RFC reaches discussion exit when the design question is settled enough to scaffold leaf items; those leaves can then be harvested by the swarm.
narratives.Background: The original docs/more-determinism.md (commit 47034134) proposed Phase 0 as 'generated per-phase task gates' but did not specify the design. v0.17.0 shipped 8 of the 12 determinism tasks but explicitly deferred Phase 0 because the design wasn't settled. This RFC closes that gap. Adjacent prior art: tasks/spec.yml exists; scripts/spec_render.py renders SPECIFICATION.md from the spec vBRIEF; skills/deft-directive-build/SKILL.md handles Phase 4 implementation. The RFC must coordinate with all three.
narratives.Constraint: MUST run a discussion phase via skills/deft-directive-discuss/SKILL.md or an equivalent design-question loop before swarm assignment. Direct swarm assignment is forbidden until the open design questions are settled. Output of discussion: an updated vBRIEF with concrete plan.items[] that ARE swarm-ready leaves.
narratives.Risk: (1) Without the discussion gate the swarm picks up an under-specified RFC and burns time on conflicting interpretations. (2) Backwards-compat with the existing speckit Phase 4 emission is non-trivial -- the RFC must not break the per-phase scope vBRIEF emission contract from #436. (3) Cross-skill coordination (build / interview) means the RFC's outcome affects multiple skill files; a multi-PR rollout is likely.
narratives.Outcome: Acceptance: (1) RFC discussion phase reaches close-out with the four open design questions settled; (2) plan.items[] in this vBRIEF are rewritten as concrete swarm-ready leaves post-discussion; (3) at least one leaf demonstrates a working `task spec:phase0` (or equivalent) for the deft repo's own spec; (4) the #233 vBRIEF item `phase-0-generated-per-phase-gates` references this RFC's outcome and unblocks; (5) `task check` passes with whatever new tasks land.
narratives.Test: Verification deferred to per-leaf items post-discussion. RFC validation: discussion notes appended to plan.narratives.Discussion; final plan.items[] reflect settled design.
narratives.Action: Pre-swarm step: run skills/deft-directive-discuss/SKILL.md (or equivalent) on this vBRIEF before any implementation work. Once discussion closes, this vBRIEF moves to vbrief/pending/ via task scope:promote, and the new leaf items become harvestable.
narratives.Urgency: This RFC is the upstream gate for 3 of the 6 remaining items in the #233 More Determinism brief: HARD-blocks `phase-0-generated-per-phase-gates` and SOFT-blocks `task-ci-local` and `task-release`. Half of the #233 tail sits idle until the discussion phase runs. Running discussion is therefore a near-term priority distinct from this brief's lifecycle position (which stays in proposed/ until discussion concludes per its own self-gate).

items.title: Run discussion phase on the 4 open design questions (one-task-per-spec vs per-phase, task location, skill interaction, speckit backwards-compat)

narrative.Action: Use skills/deft-directive-discuss/SKILL.md or an equivalent Feynman-style alignment loop. Append decisions to plan.narratives.Discussion.

items.title: Post-discussion: rewrite plan.items[] as concrete swarm-ready leaves with no remaining open design questions

narrative.Action: Each leaf must be a single PR's worth of work; no leaf may carry an open design question.

items.title: Promote this vBRIEF from proposed/ to pending/ via `task scope:promote` once leaves are concrete

narrative.Action: Lifecycle move signals readiness for swarm harvest.

x-migrator.PhaseDescription: Near-up structural priority -- post-cutover stabilization, structural unblockers (items gating many others), and cross-cutting items whose effect spans multiple skills, strategies, or files.

x-tracking.blocked_by: PR #401 trim merge: CLEARED 2026-04-28 (determinism design surface freed). RFC stays in proposed/ per its own self-gate -- promotion to pending/ is gated on discussion phase concluding the 4 open design questions, NOT on any external dependency.

references.title: Issue #635 (epic, parent): skill-system maturity + framework-rule determinism
Issue #642: PR #401 trim tracker (canonical workflow comment authorizes this RFC split)
Issue #233: More Determinism (this RFC settles the Phase 0 design question that gates the #233 phase-0-generated-per-phase-gates item)
#233 (remaining 6) -- the phase-0-generated-per-phase-gates item references this RFC as its design gate (related-plan, not parent/child, so no D4 back-link required)
